title: Intuitionism and Thought Experiments
description: Use a thought experiment to test intuitionist justification.

steps.text: You can stop the trolley by pushing a large man off a bridge, killing him to save five.

steps.prompt: Is it permissible to push the man?

steps.text: Test whether a basic constraint against killing fits your judgment.
Intuitionism holds that some basic moral propositions are self-evident.
Thought experiments help reveal and test those intuitions.

steps.notes: Check whether your intuition and principle align.
